data.narrative:
D-Orbit, a Series C space and aerospace company that has raised $166M to date, is valued against a peer universe of 5 private and 5 public comparables in the Space & Aerospace sector. The private peer set includes Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B). Public anchors span Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, AST SpaceMobile, and Satellite Investment Group, trading at a sector median P/S of 64.2x.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $166M raised proxy, implying $282M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage, same-sector private comparables yields $1.5B, with a range of $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data, typical for private companies.

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $240M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $891M. The material gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects typical dispersion in early-stage aerospace valuations and uncertainty around D-Orbit's relative market position, technology differentiation, and path to revenue within the peer cohort.
